comment_text.sn34.9:1.4: The failure of “persistence” in a meditator is often linked with their emotional hard-heartedness and mental shackles (MN 16:3.1). Without a faithful, kind, and wholesome disposition it is difficult to sustain a meditation practice.

root_text.sn34.9:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.
root_text.sn34.9:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.
root_text.sn34.9:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.
root_text.sn34.9:1.7: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca.
root_text.sn34.9:1.8: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

translation_text.sn34.9:1.4: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in practicing persistently for it. …”
